error_signature: The caller does not have permission
title: Google Sheets permission denied error
symptom: When executing a workflow with the Google Sheets node, n8n returns a '403 Forbidden' error indicating permission issues.
applies_when: Credentials are set up in n8n
not_this_if: Credentials are valid and working in other nodes
Error occurs in a production environment
root_cause: The error occurs when the Google Sheets API credentials are not properly configured, or the redirect URL used during OAuth2 authentication is set to localhost, which Google does not accept. This prevents the API from recognizing the caller's permissions.
fix_steps: Ensure that the redirect URL in your Google Cloud API credentials is set to a public URL, not localhost.
Use services like ngrok or localtunnel to expose your localhost to a public URL.
If running n8n in Docker, update the WEEBHOOK_URL environment variable to point to your public URL.
Reconnect the Google Sheets credentials in n8n after making these changes.
agent_instruction: Update the redirect URL for your Google Sheets API credentials to a public URL and reconnect the credentials in n8n.
